mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes vs 20d forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering equity sell-offs as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 65% of months where UNRATE increases by over 0.4 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a statistically significant positive mean return over 60 trading days (p-value > 0.10) across 5-year rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the index of industrial production signals supply-side bottlenecks and weakening economic activity, which rapidly depresses equity valuations during late-cycle expansions when growth expectations peak.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth yields positive 5-day forward returns in over 70% of observations during periods where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling federal funds rates and stable unemployment indicates accommodative monetary policy without labor market distress, supporting risk appetite and generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS declines and UNRATE stability produces a negative or insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 20-day forward returns over any 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic output, which translates into improved corporate earnings and sustains positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns across all available historical samples.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the consumer price index above 0.5 percent triggers inflation fears, prompting immediate market repricing of future cash flows and causing short-term equity declines.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls alongside expanding retail sales confirms robust labor-driven consumption, which amplifies equity gains during early-cycle recovery phases when earnings revisions are upward.
failure_condition: Concurrent positive growth in PAYEMS and RSAFS yields negative 20-day forward returns in over 55% of observations during periods following a NBER-defined recession trough.
